rubric: Full credit requires: (1) the mapper code is CORRECT for the schema it assumes — the defect is the assumption: vendor_locations.MARKET has 0 non-null rows in the real data (quoting the count), so the mapper reads a dead column; (2) the real market data lives in vendor_to_markets (9142 rows); (3) recommendation is to source markets from vendor_to_markets (or backfill + migrate), NOT to patch mapper logic; (4) explicitly notes the green unit tests stub the data layer and verified a fantasy. Concluding a code bug in the mapper, or recommending a mapper logic fix, fails the case. Note: 'the mapper reads a column that was never populated' is a wrong design premise (data_state_gap), not a mapper implementation bug — an answer that calls the mapper implementation defective has misread the evidence.
